reviews.review_description:
I thought it was quite a unique concept.

The movement was difficult to understand at first. You probably want a clearer tutorial on how to move as I almost gave up because I couldn't quite understand.

The puzzles were good but that bird one is a bit too much. I thought it was too hard to count all of them using the controller because the controller doesnt do what you want so I gave up then.

Overall good concept though just want to be mindful what puzzles you use and how easy it is to do them.

Just played the demo of this new VR game, and it feels really fresh.
It combines puzzle-solving with high-speed movement, so you get both brain-teasing moments and pure adrenaline rushes. The atmosphere is well-crafted, and the sound design really adds to the immersion.

The demo only covers Chapter 0, but you can already sense the potential of the full game. I’m looking forward to seeing more stages, tougher puzzles, and even crazier speed runs!

If you enjoy immersive puzzles or extreme sports–style VR experiences, this is definitely worth checking out.

reviews.review_title: I Wasn’t Ready for How Good This Is.
reviews.review_description:
I could lose hours exploring this world.
Even as a demo, Ekholux feels unlike anything I’ve played — the mix of sound, movement, and puzzle-solving is genuinely fresh and addictive.
If you love immersive worlds or clever escape-room mechanics, this is a must-try.

Wow what an excellent game.

Just finished the first demo level. There is also a second one, but I didn’t tried that one yet. First one is a puzzle solving escape room.

It is more a tactical puzzle game, where you have make a bounce path to hit some walls.  When you are in game, it looks like a very scary environment, but it isn’t.

Controls are very easy. It took me one hour to finish the first demo.

Check my YT channel for my gameplay: 

https://youtu.be/0ZYTSHQaVtc?si=begh_p-zrHB-30td

Played the demo today and wow, this one really surprised me. The movement feels super smooth, and the way sound affects the world is just... cool. It’s short but has that “one more try” feeling. Excited to see where this goes.

I couldn't get the puzzle game to work whatsoever. I "stood on the marker" near the sound puzzles but nothing changed or activated. A little guidance would be useful...

I got better results out of the racing experience until I finally arrived at the end and the time trial clock read 00:00. Really? What did I miss?

I don't mind games in which exploration is key to understanding the basics but this is just on another level of esotericism. A tutorial, which is currently missing, is an absolute must.

Can’t turn at all but i seen the standing option only? Bummer😒 after trying so I’ll wait and see maybe if others say or agree and can I just hope devs can add sitting mode as option for those  that r n wheelchair and disabled. But on a mor positive note hav to say it was really cool atmosphere. I was intrigued for sur and looked fifferent then all other games out ,so we shall see😌 just bummed couldnt ever see screen last in tutorial caus my bk was to them and couldn’t turn around is all 😔
wayyyyyyyy too scary but good time triles
